_source: TSU Fast Track Weeks 1-6, TSU Connect training
_description: Valid Business Reason (VBR) framework and templates for opening calls and emails

framework.definition: A VBR is a concise, prospect-focused reason for reaching out that demonstrates research and frames the conversation around a business outcome — not a product pitch.
framework.purpose: Get a 20-minute meeting by giving the prospect a compelling reason to invest time with you.
core_elements: Reference something specific and real about their business (not generic)
Connect it to a business outcome they care about
Ask for a short, specific meeting — not a sales call
Sound like you know the industry, not like you audited a website
what_makes_a_strong_vbr: It's specific to THIS prospect — references their actual services, location, or reputation
It leads with their world, not your product
It's brief — 2-3 sentences max before the ask
It positions you as someone doing local research, not mass outreach

subject_line_formulas: Quick question about [Business Name]'s [specific service or season]
Following up on something I noticed about [Business Name]

body_structure.opening: Reference something specific about their business — a service, a review, a promotion, or a visible opportunity. Show you did real research.
body_structure.vbr_statement: One sentence on WHY you're reaching out now — frame it around their business goal or a timing-based reason (season, competition, growth window).
body_structure.value_tease: One sentence hinting at what you've seen work for businesses like theirs — reference a result, not a product.
body_structure.ask: Clear, low-pressure request for 20 minutes. Offer two specific time options.
body_structure.signature: Your name, title, Townsquare Ignite, phone number

sample_vbr_email.subject: Quick idea for [Business Name]
sample_vbr_email.body:
Hi [First Name],

I was doing some local research on [category] businesses in [market] and [Business Name] stood out — [specific observation about their services, reputation, or a visible opportunity].

I work with businesses like yours to help them grow their customer base through targeted local digital advertising, and I had a specific idea I wanted to run by you.

Would you have 20 minutes this [Day] or [Day] to connect? Happy to keep it short and specific.

[Your Name]
Townsquare Ignite
[Your Phone]

anatomy.hook: State your name and company in the first 5 seconds — no long preamble.
anatomy.vbr: Why you're calling THIS business TODAY — specific, not generic. Reference something real.
anatomy.credibility: One sentence on who you work with or a result you've seen — without pitching a product.
anatomy.ask: Clear request for a short meeting. Give a specific time window.

phone_vbr_structure.timing: 25-35 seconds total for phone, 15-20 seconds for walk-in
phone_vbr_structure.sample_phone_script: Hi, this is [Your Name] with Townsquare Ignite — I'll be quick. I was doing local research on [category] businesses in [market] and [Business Name] came up. I had a specific idea I wanted to share about how you could [business outcome relevant to their category]. Would you have 20 minutes [day] or [day] to connect? I'll bring something specific — no fluff.
phone_vbr_structure.sample_walkin_script: Hi — I'm [Your Name] from Townsquare Ignite. I was doing local research today and [Business Name] stood out. I had a quick idea I wanted to share with whoever handles your marketing. Is [owner/manager name] available for just a few minutes?

voicemail_vbr.structure: Name → specific business detail that shows research → one-line reason to call back → callback number
voicemail_vbr.sample: Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] with Townsquare Ignite. I was doing research on [category] businesses in [market] and I had a specific idea for [Business Name] I think you'd find interesting — especially around [specific timing or growth angle]. Give me a call back at [Your Phone]. I'll keep it short.

vbr_triggers_by_situation.trigger: Business has strong reviews but limited online visibility
vbr_triggers_by_situation.vbr_angle: Your reputation is strong — the challenge is making sure more people find it before they find a competitor.

vbr_triggers_by_situation.trigger: Competitive category with multiple local players
vbr_triggers_by_situation.vbr_angle: In categories like yours, top-of-mind awareness often determines who gets the call — not who's actually better.

vbr_triggers_by_situation.trigger: Seasonal business approaching a peak window
vbr_triggers_by_situation.vbr_angle: Timing matters a lot in your category — businesses that start building awareness 6-8 weeks before their peak season see better results than those who wait.

vbr_triggers_by_situation.trigger: Business appears to rely on referrals or word of mouth only
vbr_triggers_by_situation.vbr_angle: Referral businesses are often sitting on more growth potential than they realize — there's a whole local audience looking for exactly what you do that never finds you.
New locations or new services create a window where getting the word out early sets the pace for how fast you build a customer base.

vbr_triggers_by_situation.trigger: Business is in a high-LTV category (legal, home services, medical, auto)
vbr_triggers_by_situation.vbr_angle: In your category, each new customer is worth a significant amount over time — the math on acquiring even a few new clients through digital advertising is usually very favorable.

tsi_vbr_differentiators: We work with businesses locally, not as a national agency — we understand your market
We can show you exactly how campaigns perform, not just impressions and clicks
Our full-funnel approach connects awareness to actual customer action
We've worked with businesses in [category] in markets like yours and know what moves the needle
We're a local team — you can call us and someone actually picks up
